fields.label: Cloud provider
fields.help: Cloud provider to target. GCP and Azure support planned for a future phase.

fields.label: Region
fields.help: AWS region where all resources will be provisioned.

fields.label: Credentials method
fields.help: How Terraform authenticates with AWS. Environment variables (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) is recommended for CI/CD. Shared credentials file uses ~/.aws/credentials. Static keys are embedded in config (not recommended for production).

fields.label: AWS profile name
fields.help: Required when credentials method is 'shared_credentials_file'. Name of the profile in ~/.aws/credentials.

fields.label: Access key ID
fields.help: Only used when credentials method is 'static_keys'. Not recommended for production — use environment variables or IAM roles instead.

fields.label: Secret access key
fields.help: Only used when credentials method is 'static_keys'. Store this in a secrets manager, never in version control.

fields.label: Backend type
fields.help: Where Terraform stores its state file. 'local' stores in terraform.tfstate on disk. 's3' stores in an S3 bucket — recommended for teams. 'remote' uses Terraform Cloud.

fields.label: S3 bucket name
fields.help: Name of the S3 bucket to store state. The bucket must already exist. Required when backend type is 's3'.

fields.label: State file path (key)
fields.help: Path within the S3 bucket for the state file. Use a meaningful path that includes environment and stack name.

fields.label: S3 bucket region
fields.help: Region where the S3 state bucket is located. Can differ from the provider region.

fields.label: DynamoDB lock table
fields.help: DynamoDB table name for state locking. Prevents concurrent runs from corrupting state. Recommended for team workflows.

fields.label: Encrypt state at rest
fields.help: Encrypt the state file in S3 using server-side encryption.

fields.label: Resource name
fields.help: Terraform logical identifier. Referenced by other resources as aws_vpc.<resource_name>.id. Use lowercase letters, digits, and underscores.

fields.label: CIDR block
fields.help: IPv4 CIDR block for the VPC. /16 gives 65,536 addresses. Plan your subnetting before choosing this — it cannot be changed without recreating the VPC.

fields.label: Enable DNS support
fields.help: Enables DNS resolution within the VPC. Required for Route 53 private hosted zones and ECS service discovery.

fields.label: Enable DNS hostnames
fields.help: Assigns public DNS hostnames to instances with public IPs. Required if you need to resolve EC2 instances by hostname.

fields.label: Instance tenancy
fields.help: 'default' shares hardware with other AWS accounts (standard pricing). 'dedicated' runs on single-tenant hardware (significantly higher cost — required for some compliance scenarios).

fields.label: Name tag
fields.help: Value for the Name tag visible in the AWS Console.

fields.label: Environment tag
fields.help: Standard environment tag for cost allocation and resource filtering.

fields.label: Resource name
fields.help: Unique Terraform identifier for this subnet. Convention: <type>_<az> e.g. public_1a, private_1b.

fields.label: CIDR block
fields.help: Must be a sub-range of the VPC CIDR block. /24 gives 256 addresses (251 usable — AWS reserves 5).

fields.label: Availability zone
fields.help: AZ where this subnet is created. For high availability, create subnets in at least 2 AZs.

fields.label: Auto-assign public IP
fields.help: Automatically assign a public IP to instances launched in this subnet. Enable for public subnets, disable for private subnets.

fields.label: VPC reference
fields.help: Resource name of the VPC defined in the VPC section above. The generated config will reference aws_vpc.<value>.id.

fields.label: VPC reference
fields.help: Resource name of the VPC this gateway is attached to.

fields.label: Resource name
fields.help: Unique Terraform identifier. Convention: describe the purpose e.g. web_sg, app_sg, db_sg.

fields.label: Security group name
fields.help: Name displayed in the AWS Console. Must be unique within the VPC.

fields.label: Description
fields.placeholder: Allow HTTP and HTTPS inbound traffic
fields.help: Brief description of what this security group controls.

fields.label: VPC reference
fields.help: VPC resource name. If left blank, the security group is created in the default VPC.

fields.label: Ingress — from port
fields.help: Start of inbound port range. Use 0 for all ports when protocol is -1 (all).

fields.label: Ingress — to port
fields.help: End of inbound port range. Set same as from_port for a single port.

fields.label: Ingress — protocol
fields.help: '-1' means all protocols (from/to port must be 0).

fields.label: Ingress — CIDR blocks
fields.help: Source CIDR for inbound traffic. Use 0.0.0.0/0 for public access. Restrict to known CIDRs for private services.

fields.label: Egress — protocol
fields.help: '-1' with ports 0/0 allows all outbound traffic (standard default).

fields.label: Key pair name
fields.help: Name registered in AWS. EC2 instances reference this name to allow SSH access.

fields.label: Public key material
fields.help: Contents of your public key file (.pub). Generate with: ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096

fields.label: Resource name
fields.help: Unique Terraform identifier for this instance. Referenced as aws_instance.<resource_name>.id.

fields.label: AMI ID
fields.help: Amazon Machine Image ID. Region-specific. Find the latest Amazon Linux 2023 AMI via: aws ssm get-parameter --name /aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/al2023-ami-kernel-default-x86_64

fields.label: Instance type
fields.help: t2.micro and t3.micro are free-tier eligible. t3 is newer generation and better value than t2. m5/m6i for general workloads. c5/c6i for compute-intensive. r5/r6i for memory-intensive. p3/g4dn for GPU workloads.

fields.label: Key pair name
fields.help: Name of the EC2 key pair for SSH access. Must already exist in AWS or be defined in the Key Pair section above.

fields.label: Subnet reference
fields.help: Resource name of the subnet (defined in the Subnet section). The generated config will use aws_subnet.<value>.id.

fields.label: Security group references
fields.help: Comma-separated resource names of security groups from the Security Group section. e.g. web_sg, app_sg

fields.label: Associate public IP
fields.help: Assign a public IP address to this instance. Only effective in a public subnet with internet gateway attached.

fields.label: Root volume size (GB)
fields.help: Size of the root EBS volume in GB. Minimum varies by AMI (typically 8GB). Increasing size is non-destructive; shrinking requires manual migration.

fields.label: Root volume type
fields.help: gp3 is current-gen SSD, better performance and lower cost than gp2 (recommended default). io1/io2 for high IOPS databases. st1 for throughput-intensive big data. sc1 for cold storage.

fields.label: EBS optimized
fields.help: Dedicates network bandwidth between the instance and EBS. Enabled by default on most modern instance types.

fields.label: Detailed monitoring
fields.help: Enable detailed CloudWatch monitoring (1-minute intervals). Basic monitoring (5-minute intervals) is free. Detailed monitoring incurs additional cost.

fields.label: IAM instance profile
fields.help: IAM instance profile ARN or name to attach to this instance. Grants the instance AWS service permissions without embedding credentials.

fields.label: User data script
fields.placeholder:
#!/bin/bash
yum update -y
yum install -y httpd
systemctl start httpd
fields.help: Shell script executed once on first boot. Use for bootstrapping software, configuring the OS, or registering with a config management tool.

fields.label: Resource name
fields.help: Unique Terraform identifier.

fields.label: Instance reference
fields.help: Resource name of the EC2 instance to associate with. Leave blank to allocate an unattached EIP.

fields.label: Bucket name
fields.help: Globally unique S3 bucket name. Must be 3–63 characters, lowercase, no underscores. Convention: <org>-<purpose>-<environment>.

fields.label: Enable versioning
fields.help: Keeps all versions of every object. Required for cross-region replication and protects against accidental deletion. Increases storage costs.

fields.label: Server-side encryption
fields.help: AES256 uses S3-managed keys (SSE-S3) — no additional cost. aws:kms uses Customer Managed Keys — provides audit trail and access control at the key level.

fields.label: Block public ACLs
fields.help: Prevents public access via ACLs. Recommended ON for all buckets unless serving public static assets.

fields.label: Force destroy
fields.help: Allow Terraform to delete the bucket even if it contains objects. Set to true only in non-production environments.

fields.label: DB identifier
fields.help: Unique identifier for the RDS instance within your account and region. Used in the DNS endpoint hostname.

fields.label: Database engine
fields.help: Database engine. Aurora variants are AWS-managed clustered versions with automatic failover.

fields.label: Engine version
fields.help: Engine version number. Check AWS documentation for supported versions. Use a recent minor version for security patches.

fields.label: Instance class
fields.help: db.t3.micro is free-tier eligible (750 hours/month). t4g instances offer better price-performance. m5/m6g for general workloads. r5/r6g for memory-intensive workloads.

fields.label: Allocated storage (GB)
fields.help: Initial storage in GB. Minimum is 20GB for gp2/gp3. Can be increased without downtime.

fields.label: Max allocated storage (GB)
fields.help: Upper limit for RDS autoscaling storage. Leave blank to disable autoscaling. Set to a value > allocated_storage to enable.

fields.label: Storage type
fields.help: gp3 is the recommended default — better performance and lower cost than gp2. io1 for high-throughput production databases requiring >16,000 IOPS.

fields.label: Encrypt storage
fields.help: Encrypts data at rest using AES-256. Required for most compliance frameworks (PCI-DSS, HIPAA, SOC2).

fields.label: Initial database name
fields.help: Name of the database created on first launch. Leave blank if you'll create databases manually after provisioning.

fields.label: Master username
fields.help: Master database user. Do not use 'admin', 'root', or 'postgres' — AWS reserves these. Store credentials in AWS Secrets Manager.

fields.label: Multi-AZ deployment
fields.help: Creates a synchronous standby replica in a different AZ. Automatic failover within ~1-2 minutes. Required for production workloads. Doubles the instance cost.

fields.label: Publicly accessible
fields.help: Makes the DB endpoint reachable from outside the VPC. Never enable in production — access through a bastion host or VPN instead.

fields.label: Backup retention (days)
fields.help: Number of days to retain automated backups. 0 disables backups. Minimum 7 days recommended for production. Maximum 35 days.

fields.label: Skip final snapshot
fields.help: When true, Terraform can delete the DB without creating a final backup. Set to true only for dev/test environments.

fields.label: Security group references
fields.help: Comma-separated resource names of security groups from the Security Group section.

fields.label: Load balancer name
fields.help: Name for the load balancer. 1-32 characters, alphanumeric and hyphens only. Cannot start or end with a hyphen.

fields.label: Type
fields.help: Application (ALB): HTTP/HTTPS, path/host-based routing, WebSockets. Network (NLB): TCP/UDP, ultra-low latency, static IPs. Gateway: third-party virtual appliances.

fields.label: Internal (private)
fields.help: Internal LBs are only reachable within the VPC. Use for service-to-service communication. Internet-facing LBs have public DNS names.

fields.label: Subnet references
fields.help: Comma-separated subnet resource names. ALBs require at least 2 subnets in different AZs.

fields.label: Security group references
fields.help: Comma-separated security group resource names. Required for ALBs, not applicable for NLBs.

fields.label: Deletion protection
fields.help: Prevents accidental deletion of the load balancer. Enable in production environments.

fields.label: Role name
fields.help: IAM role name. Use a descriptive PascalCase name describing the service and purpose. e.g. ECSTaskExecutionRole, LambdaS3ReadRole.

fields.label: Description
fields.placeholder: Allows EC2 instances to call AWS services

fields.label: Trusted service
fields.help: The AWS service that can assume this role. EC2 for instance profiles, ecs-tasks for ECS containers, lambda for Lambda functions.

fields.label: Managed policy ARNs
fields.help: One AWS managed or customer managed policy ARN per line. Find ARNs in IAM Console > Policies. Follow least-privilege — attach only required permissions.

fields.label: Log group name
fields.help: Log group path. Convention: /<app>/<environment>/<component>. AWS services use their own convention e.g. /ecs/<cluster>/<service>.

fields.label: Retention (days)
fields.help: How long CloudWatch retains log events. 0 means never expire (logs retained indefinitely — higher cost). Balance compliance requirements with storage cost.

fields.label: KMS key ID
fields.help: Optional KMS key ARN for encrypting log data at rest. Leave blank for default CloudWatch encryption.

fields.label: Output name
fields.help: Identifier for this output. Referenced by other Terraform modules as module.<name>.<output_name>.

fields.label: Description
fields.placeholder: Public IP address of the web server

fields.label: Value expression
fields.help: Terraform expression to output. Examples: aws_instance.web_server.public_ip | aws_lb.app_alb.dns_name | aws_db_instance.app_db.endpoint

fields.label: Mark as sensitive
fields.help: Sensitive outputs are redacted in Terraform plan/apply output. Use for passwords, keys, and connection strings.
